message:
feat(core): add mntent parser/serializer module (bd-mn-1)

Lifts the byte-level fstab/mtab line logic out of
frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (~150 lines of inline parsing +
NUL packing in getmntent_r/hasmntopt/addmntent) into a pure-safe
frankenlibc-core::mntent module operating on byte slices.

Public surface:
  pub struct MntFields<'a> { fsname, dir, mtype, opts, freq, passno }
  pub fn parse_mntent_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<MntFields<'_>>
  pub fn parse_mntent_freq_passno(freq_s: &[u8], passno_s: &[u8]) -> (i32, i32)
  pub fn has_mnt_opt(opts: &[u8], needle: &[u8]) -> Option<usize>
  pub fn format_mntent_line(fields: &MntFields, out: &mut Vec<u8>)

The parser:
  - tolerates trailing \n / \r\n
  - skips blank lines and comment lines (# after leading whitespace)
  - collapses runs of space/tab as separators
  - returns None for fewer than four fields
  - defaults missing freq/passno to 0
  - falls back to 0 on non-numeric trailing fields (matches glibc)

has_mnt_opt does proper token boundary checking — bounded by ',' or
string start/end — so 'exec' does NOT match inside 'noexec'. Empty
needle never matches.

format_mntent_line writes signed decimals via in-place itoa
(handles i32::MIN correctly via i64 widening), appends rather than
overwrites, and produces a byte-exact round-trip with the parser.

26 unit tests in parse.rs cover all edge cases including a real
/proc/mounts tmpfs line.

Sub-bead 2 (bd-mn-2) will rewire the abi shims to delegate to this
module, keeping the C-ABI struct marshalling at the boundary.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+//! `<mntent.h>` — fstab/mtab line parsing and serialization.
+//!
+//! Pure-safe Rust port of the byte-level logic that previously lived
+//! inline in `frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs::getmntent_r` /
+//! `hasmntopt` / `addmntent`. The abi layer keeps responsibility for
+//! the `struct mntent` field-pointer marshalling, caller-buffer NUL
+//! packing, and `BufReader`/`Write` integration; this module produces
+//! and consumes byte slices only.
+//!
+//! An mtab/fstab line has six whitespace-separated fields:
+//! `fsname dir type opts [freq [passno]]`. Lines beginning with `#`
+//! (after leading whitespace) and blank lines are comments and yield
+//! `None` from [`parse_mntent_line`].
+
+pub mod parse;
+
+pub use parse::{
+    MntFields, format_mntent_line, has_mnt_opt, parse_mntent_freq_passno, parse_mntent_line,
+};
